# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure resources that use service endpoints for connectivity. Enable service endpoints for Storage and SQL on our VNet but allow public access as fallback. This provides flexibility for accessing resources from both Azure and on-premises during migration.
# Category: cloud_network_security

parameters:
  virtualNetworkName: '<your_vnet>'   # Provide your vnetwork name here   
  subnetPrefixes:                 
      - prefixSize: 16                # Azure reserves first 20 addresses for network, last one is used as endpoint. So we use rest of the address space (prefixsize-3) to accommodate azure and onpremises resources  
        name: 'AzureSubnet'           
    - prefixSize: 19                   # Reserving top few IPv4 addresses in this subnet for public access during fallback. So, we use rest of the address space (prefixsize-2) to accommodate Azure and onpremises resources  
      name: 'OnPremSubnet'    
variables:   
  vNetAddressSpace : '/subscriptions/<your_subscription>/resourceGroups/<your_rg>/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/<your_vnetworkname>/addressSpace'   # Provide your VNET name here, you can get these from Azure portal   
  vSubnets: '[AzureSubnet], [OnPremSubnet]']                # Define all the sub-names of virtual networks to avoid typos during deployment. Do not change if already defined in azur template    
  
resources:[..., {name:'defaultRoute', type'Microsoft.Network/routeTables#addonroutetableVNetGateway','dependsOn':['<your_vnet>']}] #Add a default route table with internet gateway to the VNET in order for all traffic from Azure and On-Premises resources come back via this
  output:            
    type:'Microsoft.Network/virtualNetwork'          // Change resource provider as per your requirement, if not same then make sure that you are providing correct ARM template format (providers -> Microsoft.xxx)       
   parameters:    
      vnetName : '<your_vnetworkname>'              # Provide name of VNET  here    , can get from Azure portal or dynamically set during deployment/scripting by you using variables as provided in above step         var[each].outputs.virtualNetworkName   // Use this to access the output parameters later, it should match with parameter names passed at time ARM will execute script    
      subnet:  '[[AzureSubnet], [OnPremSubnet]]'    # Provide list of all created Subnets here from your above step         var[each].outputs.subnet   // Use this to access the output parameters later, it should match with parameter names passed at time ARM will execute script
